---
title: Ultimate Environment Construction Guide
subtitle: Complete setup for comparative genomics analysis
jupyter: python3
---

## Info Summary

This is the comprehensive environment setup guide for the comparative genomics analysis project. It provides step-by-step instructions for setting up all required bioinformatics tools and dependencies.

### Project Information
- **Docker Image**: `huang/comparative-genomics:ultimate` (after complete setup)
- **Jupyter Access**: `http://localhost:38888/?token=[your-token]`

### Available Conda Environments
After completing this setup, you will have the following environments:
- `base` - Base conda environment  
- `env_biopython` - Python environment with BioPython and analysis libraries
- `env_orthofinder` - OrthoFinder for orthology analysis
- `bioconductor-clusterprofiler_env` - R environment for functional enrichment analysis
- `eggnog_env` - EggNOG mapper for functional annotation
- `pastml_env` - PastML for ancestral state reconstruction  
- `gff2bed_env` - BEDOPS utilities for format conversion

### Available Jupyter Kernels
- `env_biopython` - Python (Biopython) kernel
- `ir` - Base R kernel
- `julia-1.7` - Julia kernel
- `python3` - Base Python kernel
- `ir_clusterprofiler_481_env` - clusterprofiler R kernel

## Environment List Check

First, let's check the current environment setup:

```{python}
!hostname
!mamba env list
!jupyter kernelspec list
```

## 1. Install OrthoFinder (3.0.1b1)

OrthoFinder is used for phylogenetic orthology inference and comparative genomics analysis.

```{python}
!mamba create -n env_orthofinder -y bioconda::orthofinder
```

Verify the installation:

```{python}
!conda run -n env_orthofinder orthofinder -h
```

## 2. Install Biopython Environment

Set up the main Python environment for bioinformatics analysis.

### 2.1 Create Base Environment (1.85)

```{python}
%%bash
mamba create -n env_biopython -y conda-forge::biopython pip ipykernel
conda run -n env_biopython python -m ipykernel install --user --name env_biopython --display-name "Python (Biopython)"
```

### 2.2 Install GFF Utilities (0.13)

```{python}
!mamba install -n env_biopython -y bioconda::gffutils
```

### 2.3 Install Data Analysis Libraries

Install essential Python libraries for data manipulation and visualization:

```{python}
!mamba install -y -n env_biopython matplotlib
```

> 3.10.0

```{python}
!mamba install -y -n env_biopython pandas
```

> 2.2.3

```{python}
!mamba install -y -n env_biopython seaborn
```

> 0.13.2

```{python}
!mamba install -y -n env_biopython scikit-learn
```

### 2.4 Install Specialized Plotting Libraries (0.9.0)

```{python}
!mamba install -y -n env_biopython conda-forge::upsetplot
```

## 3. Install clusterProfiler (R Environment)

clusterProfiler is used for functional enrichment analysis of gene clusters.

### 3.1 Search Available Versions

```{python}
!mamba search bioconda::bioconductor-clusterprofiler 
```

### 3.2 Create clusterProfiler Environment

```{python}
!mamba create --name bioconductor-clusterprofiler_env -y bioconda::bioconductor-clusterprofiler=4.8.1
```

### 3.3 Install R Jupyter Kernel (1.3.2)

```{python}
!/opt/conda/envs/bioconductor-clusterprofiler_env/bin/Rscript -e "install.packages('IRkernel', repos = 'https://ftp.yz.yamagata-u.ac.jp/pub/cran/')"
```

```{python}
%%bash
/opt/conda/envs/bioconductor-clusterprofiler_env/bin/Rscript - <<EOF
library(IRkernel)
packageVersion("IRkernel")
EOF
```

### 3.4 Register Kernel and Verify

```{python}
!/opt/conda/envs/bioconductor-clusterprofiler_env/bin/Rscript -e "IRkernel::installspec(name = 'ir_clusterprofiler_481_env', displayname = 'clusterprofiler R', user = FALSE)"
!jupyter kernelspec list
```

### 3.5 Install tools for plotting
```{r}
install.packages("phytools",repos="https://cloud.r-project.org",quiet=TRUE)
```

> also installing the dependencies ‘iterators’, ‘quadprog’, ‘maps’, ‘clusterGeneration’, ‘coda’, ‘combinat’, ‘DEoptim’, ‘doParallel’, ‘expm’, ‘foreach’, ‘mnormt’, ‘numDeriv’, ‘optimParallel’, ‘phangorn’, ‘scatterplot3d’

## 4. Install EggNOG Mapper

EggNOG mapper provides functional annotation of genes and proteins.

### 4.1 Create Environment (2.1.12)

```{python}
!mamba create -n eggnog_env -y -c bioconda -c conda-forge eggnog-mapper
```

### 4.2 Download EggNOG Database (5.0.2)

```{python}
!mkdir -p "./DB/Eggnog"
%conda run -n eggnog_env download_eggnog_data.py -y --data_dir "./DB/Eggnog"
```

## 5. Install PastML

PastML is used for ancestral state reconstruction on phylogenetic trees.

### 5.1 Create Python Environment

```{python}
print("Installing PastML...")
!mamba create -n pastml_env -y python=3.10
```

```{python}
!conda run -n pastml_env python3 --version
```

### 5.2 Install PastML via pip (1.9.50)

```{python}
!conda run -n pastml_env python3 -m pip install pastml
```

### 5.3 Verify Installation

```{python}
!conda run -n pastml_env which pastml
```

### 5.4 Clone PastML Repository for Examples

```{python}
!ls Apps
```

```{python}
!git clone https://github.com/evolbioinfo/pastml.git Apps/PastML
```

### 5.5 Test PastML with Example Data

```{python}
!conda run -n pastml_env pastml --tree ./Apps/PastML/examples/Albania/data/Albanian.tree.152tax.tre --data ./Apps/PastML/examples/Albania/data/data.txt --columns Country --html_compressed ./Test/Albanian_map.html --data_sep , 
```

## 6. Install MCScanX

MCScanX is used for synteny and collinearity analysis.

### 6.1 Install Java Dependency

```{python}
!mamba install -y conda-forge::openjdk
```

### 6.2 Download MCScanX Source

```{bash}
%%bash
ls ./Apps
mkdir -p ./Apps/MCScanX
wget https://github.com/wyp1125/MCScanX/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.0.tar.gz -O ./Apps/MCScanX/v1.0.0.tar.gz
```

### 6.3 Compile MCScanX

```{bash}
%%bash
mkdir ./Apps/MCScanX/V1
tar zxvf ./Apps/MCScanX/v1.0.0.tar.gz -C ./Apps/MCScanX/V1
cd ./Apps/MCScanX/V1/MCScanX-1.0.0; make
```

### 6.4 Verify Installation

```{bash}
%%bash
ls -lth ./Apps/MCScanX/V1/MCScanX-1.0.0
ls -lth ./Apps/MCScanX/V1/MCScanX-1.0.0/downstream_analyses
```

### 6.5 Test MCScanX Tools

```{python}
!./Apps/MCScanX/V1/MCScanX-1.0.0/duplicate_gene_classifier -h
```

## 7. Install BLAST+

BLAST+ is used for sequence similarity searches.

### 7.1 Download and Install BLAST+

```{python}
!mkdir -p ./Apps/Blast
!wget https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/blast/executables/blast+/2.16.0/ncbi-blast-2.16.0+-x64-linux.tar.gz -O ./Apps/Blast/blast-2.16.0.tar.gz
!tar zxvf ./Apps/Blast/blast-2.16.0.tar.gz -C ./Apps/Blast
!ls ./Apps/Blast/ncbi-blast-2.16.0+/bin/
```

## 8. Install BEDOPS (gff2bed)

BEDOPS provides utilities for genomic interval operations and format conversion.

### 8.1 Create BEDOPS Environment

```{python}
!mamba create -n gff2bed_env -y bioconda::bedops
```

### 8.2 Verify Installation

```{python}
!conda run -n gff2bed_env which gff2bed
```

## Installation Complete

Your comparative genomics analysis environment is now fully configured with all necessary tools:

### Core Analysis Tools
- **OrthoFinder**: Orthology inference and comparative genomics
- **MCScanX**: Synteny and collinearity analysis  
- **BLAST+**: Sequence similarity searches
- **PastML**: Ancestral state reconstruction

### Functional Annotation
- **EggNOG Mapper**: Gene/protein functional annotation
- **clusterProfiler**: Functional enrichment analysis (R)

### Data Processing & Visualization
- **BioPython**: Python bioinformatics libraries
- **Pandas, NumPy**: Data manipulation
- **Matplotlib, Seaborn**: Visualization
- **UpSetPlot**: Set intersection visualization
- **BEDOPS**: Genomic interval operations

### Development Environment
- **Jupyter Lab/Notebook**: Interactive analysis
- **Multiple kernels**: Python, R, Julia support
- **Conda environments**: Isolated tool dependencies

You can now proceed with your comparative genomics analysis workflows using these properly configured environments.
